rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELMO LEE CARTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/17/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) The proof of service filed on 5/1/23 shows that the Notice of Hearing did not have the full caption of the pleading in item 1. (5) The petition was reviewed cursorily online as a ONE-TIME courtesy. It does not appear to be a proper accounting in any event (and no bank statement was filed to verify the ending balance, as required by LRSF 14.34 D.), and the relief sought appears not to be a distribution of the estate to the heirs but a claim by Elvin Carter to assets held by Hattie Ross (Probate Code Section 850). (6) It is also unclear how relief even under Probate Code Section 850 would apply in this estate, if assets are in the name of Hattie Ross. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/7/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF STANLEY CHONG KWONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/07/2023 (no earlier date available). 1) No proposed order has been received. 2) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 3) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(D)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form. 4) Notice must be given to the former attorneys of record for the petitioner. CRC 7.704. If the former attorneys agreed to waive statutory compensation, a signed waiver must be filed. 5) Even if statutory compensation is not sought, the amount of compensation payable must be stated with its calculation. CRC 7.705. 6) All the information required by CRC 7.403 must be provided as to each creditor claim. 7) Has the petitioner taken any IAEA actions reportable under CRC 7.250? 8) Was the decedent's LLC interest (listed on the inventory and appraisal partial no. 2) his separate property, community property, or both? 9) What is the amount of the estate cash on hand representing the decedent's separate property? 10) What is the amount of the estate cash on hand representing the decedent's one-half share of community property? 11) If the petitioner is the sole trustee of the devisee trust established by the decedent and petitioner, all beneficiaries of the trust entitled to notice under Probate Code section 1208(b) must be identified. 12) Please provide a copy of a court order that authorized the stipulated termination of the decedent's separate property trust. If no court authorization has been obtained, please address the trustee's authority to terminate the trust. 13) If the decedent's separate property trust has been terminated, does Article 3 of the will require a testamentary trust to be established on the same terms and conditions? 14) The information required by CRC 7.550(b)(2) and (3) must be provided as to each non-cash asset inventoried and appraised (but not part of the assets on hand), even if an account has been waived. 15) Is the estate solvent? CRC 7.550(b)(5). Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF JASON KENNETH CHEUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/19/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of the Notice of Hearing has been filed. [Probate Code Sections 2621, 1460, and 1460.1.] (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) Original bank (or brokerage) statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] Copies attached to a petition are not sufficient. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (For interim accountings, only the statement verifying the ending balance(s) need to be filed.) (4) It appears that bond might not be adequate now. [Probate Code Section 2320 and CRC 7.207 and LRSF 14.16.] The guardian of the estate must file an ex parte petition to increase her bond. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 7/10/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Why are there so many references to guardians and petitioners when there is just one guardian? The dates orders were filed are not necessarily the dates the orders were signed. When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF VIRGINIA P CLAUDIO **TRANSFFERED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY**
rulings.Calendar Matter: FUTURE ACCOUNTING
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

(Issues should be addressed in a supplement or an amendment to a petition (or a completely amended petition) not in response to an "examiner's questions". When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES T. PORRAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/10/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order was received by the assigned examiner by the deadline of 5/4/23. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] Proposed orders are SUBMITTED in hard copy, not filed, as specifically set forth in LRSF 14.59 E. (An order is not filed until a judge signs it.) (2) Still NO COURTESY COPIES have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] DEADLINE is 6/28/23 for delivering the hard copy proposed ORDER and endorsed filed courtesy copies of ALL FILED PAPERS to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HARUWN K. WESLEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/10/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (4) No proof of publication has been filed yet. (5) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (6) The register of actions reflects only one waiver of bond by the petitioner, not also by the other heir. (7) If the petitioner is the surviving spouse, is a Spousal Property Petition not appropriate, or are all assets the decedent's separate property? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/29/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
